svg.text: Live TV Application — Host Software Architecture (Broadcast)
Android flavour · layers: Middleware / AL — Platform / PAL / OS — Drivers / HAL (OTT path omitted)
Comedia DTV Middleware (iWedia · bound via Binder)

g.text: Android / platform blocks used by Live TV
Present but not in broadcast Live-TV focus
Broadcast data / control flow (zap to service)
getLiveRoute() · Xserv orchestrates · Table parses PAT/PMT
Tuner.tune(frontendSettings) → JNI → TRM.requestFrontend → Tuner HAL: IFrontend.tune / IDemux.openFilter
Magnum / driver register writes & IRQs → TUNER · DEMUX · VDEC · ADEC · GPU(Display)
⟵ async events (return path): tunerLocked · channelChangeStatus · safeToUnblank · MAL_SZ_NotifyServiceStarted → "Service started"

svg.text: Broadcast data-flow — call chain "Zap to a Live TV service" (maps directly to the UML sequence diagram)
References: Module 7 (Broadcast Zapping / Channel Scanning sequences), Module 6 (Broadcast Tune & A/V Playback sequences), Module 5 (call-stack across layers), liba4tv / Comedia API.

g.text: Live TV App → Broadcast TV Input (TIS). User selects a channel. TIF delivers the service-level activation callback onTune(channelUri) (plus onSetSurface, onSetStreamVolume). "Tune" here = "activate this service", not RF tuning.

Broadcast TV Input → Comedia API (Service, Java). TIS resolves the channel to the service triplet and calls the bound Comedia service: setCurrentServiceByTriplet(ONID,TSID,SID) → ServiceControl.startServiceByTriplet(routeID,list,svc,onid,tsid,sid).

Comedia Service (Java) → MAL (native). Crosses the JNI boundary into native middleware. The MAL posts the async zapper request: MAL_SZ_StartTripletService() → main zapper task (returns immediately; result arrives later as an event).

MAL → Comedia MW core (Route/Xserv, Service Control, Table). Obtains the live route ComediaRouteManagerControl.getLiveRoute(...); Xserv orchestrates the signal path; the Table module parses PAT/PMT to discover A/V + PCR PIDs; services stored in SList + PIDB.

Comedia MW → Comedia CHAL (glue / TDAL_*). The platform-independent core calls its glue API: TDAL_DMD (frontend/input), TDAL_DMX (demux / PID filtering), TDAL_AV (decoders). CHAL hides whether the target is Android Tuner SDK or vendor Nexus.

CHAL → Tuner SDK (clean Android-aligned PAL path). Tuner.tune(frontendSettings) → JNI → Tuner Native API → TRM.requestFrontend (resource grant) → Tuner HAL IFrontend.tune / IDemux.openFilter. A/V decode via MediaCodec + AudioTrack.

CHAL / Tuner HAL → Vendor DTV SDK (Broadcom NEXUS). NEXUS_Frontend_Tune → build the filter graph: InputBand → ParserBand → PidChannel, then VideoDecoder/AudioDecoder_SetInput+Start, StcChannel (PCR lip-sync), VideoWindow→Display.

NEXUS / Magnum → Drivers / HAL. Vendor layer performs memory-mapped register writes, buffer setup and IRQ handling against the real blocks: TUNER, DEMUX, VDEC, ADEC, GPU/Display. (In Cuttlefish a TS file feeds the input instead of a real tuner — same abstraction.)

Asynchronous return path (dashed). Events propagate back up: tunerLocked / lock event → channelChangeStatus + safeToUnblank (ServiceListener) → MAL_SZ_NotifyServiceStarted → Comedia Service → TIS "Service started" → Live TV App. Volume is unmuted; playback active.

Note — the same spine is reused for Service installation (scan): steps 1–7 become startScanByUrl → ScanControl.autoScan(routeID,url) → getInstallRoute() → TDAL_*_Tune (file/frontend) → Table parses PAT/PMT/SDT → installStatus/scanProgressChanged/scanFinished events, then services are written into SList+PIDB and published to the Android TV Provider.

Note — one block-to-block arrow is not necessarily one API call: a single middleware call (e.g. startServiceByTriplet) expands into many SDK + driver calls underneath (Module 5: "call stack across the layers").
